content.full_text:
RAy! MERTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES
COMMISSION
July 18,
1995
Mr. William S.
Bradley
Supervisor, District I
Board of Supervisors
County of El Dorado
330. Fair Lane
Placerville, California 95667
Re:
Your Request For Advice
Our File No.
A—95-208
Dear Mr.
Bradley:
We respond to your request for advice regarding your duties
as a county supervisor under the conflict—of—interest provisions
of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).
1
QUESTION
May you participate as a member of the Local Agency Formation
Commission in a decision concerning whether the Cameron Park Fire
Department should be annexed into the El Dorado Hills County Water
District when you own a home in Cameron Park?
ANSWER
Yes.
Based on the facts presented to us, there is no
- reasonablyforeseeable financial effect on your -property interest
arising out of that decision.
-Therefore, there is no conflict of
interest under the Act.
FACTS
You are a .member of the El Dorado County Board of
Supervisors.
You also sit as a member of the Local Agency
Formation Commission.
The Local Agency Formation Commission will
decide several questions related to whether the Cameron Park Fire
Department will be annexed into the El Dorado Hills County Water
District.
The annexation will benefit both Cameron Park and El
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
. Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations, Sections
18000—18995.
428 J STREET, SU1TE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660


File No.
A—95—208
Page
2
Dorado Hills.
Cameron Park, however,
may lose its power to
oversee the firefighting services as El Dorado Hills continues to
grow in the number of its residents.
ANALYSIS
The conflict—of-interest provisions of the Act prohibit a
public official from making, participating in making,
or otherwise
attempting to influence a governmental decision in which the
official knows or has reason to know that he has a financial
interest.
(Section 87100.)
An official has a financial interest
in a governmental decision, within the meaning of the Act, if it
is reasonably foreseeable that a decision in which the official
participates will have a material financial effect,
distinguishable from the effect on the public generally,
on any
one of five enumerated economic interests,
including, real
property in which the official has a direct or indirect interest
worth $1,000 or more.
(Section 87103.)
You have a residence located in Cameron Park.
Although the
annexation of the Cameron Park Fire Department with the El Dorado
Hills County Water District may be of benefit to both areas and
may cause Cameron Park residents to lose oversight control over
their fire department to the larger population center of El Dorado
Hills,
any financial effect on your residence appears to be remote
and not reasonably foreseeable.
Reasonable foreseeability is a question of fact.
For a
financial effect to be reasonably foreseeable it must’be
substantially likely to occur.
Absolute certainty is not
required.
However,
a mere possibility is insufficient to rise to
the level of reasonable foreseeability.
(In re Thorner
(1975)
1
FPPC Ops.
198.)
The change in the organization and structure of
the firefighting organization serving Camepn Park appears,
from
the facts you have provided, to be financially neutral, neithex
raising nor lowering the taxes Cameron Park residents pay for
their services.
Accordingly, there is no reasonably foreseeable
financial effect on your home.
Any effect caused by a future
change in the level of services provided to Cameron Park, caused
by the increased demand for services the larger population in
El DoradoHills may require,
isE too remote to be considered
reasonably foreseeable.
Accordingly, there is no conflict of interest in your
participation in the Local Agency Formation Commission decision
regarding the annexation of the Cameron Park Fire Department into


File No.
A—95—208
Page
3
the El Dorado Hills County Water District.
Should you have any
questions regarding this advice, please contact the undersigned.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
Daniel E.
Mualleiu
Counsel,
Legal Division
SGC:DEM:ak
